The Pod
← Home
AI-Generated Podcast — Learn on the go

Every lesson, as a conversation.

Each lesson from every track gets broken down into a conversational podcast episode. Two AI hosts discuss the concepts, debate the edge cases, and make the ideas stick — so you can learn while you walk, drive, or zone out.

How it works

Step 01

Lesson goes in

Each written lesson from D3, Django, or SQL gets fed into NotebookLM as source material.

Step 02

Podcast comes out

Two AI hosts have a natural conversation about the topic, explaining concepts through back-and-forth dialogue.

Step 03

Listen anywhere

Play episodes directly in the browser, or download them. Each one is 8–15 minutes of focused learning.

D3.js Episodes

01

SVG Fundamentals

The coordinate system, basic shapes, and why SVG is D3's canvas.

D3 ~12 min
02

Selections & Data Binding

How D3 connects data to DOM elements — the wedding seating chart mental model.

D3 ~14 min
03

Scales & Axes

Translating data values into visual positions. Linear, band, time, and color scales.

D3 ~11 min
04

Shapes & Data Joins

Enter, update, exit — the core pattern for dynamic data-driven graphics.

D3 ~13 min
05

Maps & Geography

Projections, GeoJSON, TopoJSON, and building choropleth maps for broadcast.

D3 ~15 min

React Episodes

01

The React Mental Model

Components, state, and the declarative paradigm — thinking in React from scratch.

React ~13 min
02

Hooks & State

useState, useEffect, and the rules of hooks. Why order matters.

React ~14 min
03

Component Patterns

Composition, render props, and building reusable UI pieces.

React ~12 min

AI & LLM Episodes

01

How LLMs Work

Tokens, attention, and the transformer architecture — demystified for builders.

AI ~14 min
02

Prompt Engineering

System prompts, few-shot examples, and chain-of-thought reasoning.

AI ~11 min
03

RAG & Embeddings

Retrieval-augmented generation, vector databases, and semantic search.

AI ~13 min

Django Episodes

01

Python Fundamentals

Variables, types, and control flow — explained through JavaScript analogies.

Django ~10 min
02

Python Collections

Lists, dicts, sets, and tuples. How they map to JS arrays and objects.

Django ~12 min
03

Python Functions

Decorators, generators, comprehensions — Python's power tools.

Django ~11 min

SQL Episodes

01

SQL Fundamentals

Tables, rows, columns, and the relational model. Why SQL still runs the world.

SQL ~10 min
02

SELECT & Filtering

WHERE, LIKE, IN, BETWEEN — the query fundamentals that do 80% of the work.

SQL ~9 min
03

Joins

INNER, LEFT, RIGHT, FULL — connecting tables like connecting stories.

SQL ~13 min

The Pod — part of Learn Coding by Trevor Brown
